target.title: fix(sessions): avoid cross-cwd recent resumes
target.body:
Closes #96542

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users resuming a recent session from one project could silently load another project's transcript when the two project paths encode to the same default session directory, such as `/home/alice/dev/client/app` and `/home/alice/dev/client-app`.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix keeps the existing shipped session-directory encoding intact and makes recent-session selection cwd-aware instead. `continueRecent` now only resumes JSONL files whose persisted session header `cwd` matches the requested cwd, and the normal per-project session list uses the same header-cwd filter. `listAll` remains unchanged because it is intentionally cross-project.

This avoids orphaning existing session directories while preventing a colliding directory from adopting the newest transcript for a different project.

## User Impact

Continuing a session from a project now resumes only that project's transcript, or starts fresh when no matching session exists. Users no longer get another project's conversation history mixed into the current cwd when path separators and literal hyphens collide in the encoded session directory.

## Evidence

- Duplicate PR gate:
  - `gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 50 --json number,title,url,updatedAt,body -- "96542 OR \"project cwds differing\" OR \"getDefaultSessionDir\" OR \"client-app\""` -> `[]`
- Real on-disk resume-path proof for the CLI/gateway session-manager boundary:
  - Command: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from this branch, using the production `getDefaultSessionDir`, `findMostRecentSession`, `SessionManager.continueRecent`, and `SessionManager.list` exports against two real JSONL session headers in one isolated temp agent dir.
  - Observed terminal output, redacted only for the temp root:

```text
OpenClaw cwd-collision resume proof
agent dir: <tmp>/agent
cwd A: /home/alice/dev/client/app
cwd B: /home/alice/dev/client-app
default dirs collide: true
session A file: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-00-000Z_session-a.jsonl
session B file: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-01-000Z_session-b.jsonl
unfiltered newest file: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-01-000Z_session-b.jsonl
cwd-filtered newest for cwd A: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-00-000Z_session-a.jsonl
continueRecent(cwd A) file: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-00-000Z_session-a.jsonl
continueRecent(cwd A) header cwd: /home/alice/dev/client/app
continueRecent(cwd A) crossed cwd: false
SessionManager.list(cwd A) files: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-00-000Z_session-a.jsonl
SessionManager.list(cwd A) cwds: /home/alice/dev/client/app
```

  - Claim proved: the two example cwds still collide under the shipped directory encoding, but the fixed real resume selector does not adopt the newer JSONL file from the other cwd; `continueRecent(cwd A)` resumes only the session whose header `cwd` is `cwd A`, and `SessionManager.list(cwd A)` filters out the other cwd.
- Focused regression test:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 48 tests passed.
- Touched-file formatting/lint:
  - `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
  - `node_modules\.bin\oxlint.CMD src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts` -> exit 0.
- Patch hygiene:
  - `git diff --check` -> clean.
  - `git diff --cached --check` -> clean before commit.
- Autoreview:
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` -> `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Not tested / proof gaps:

- I did not run a provider-backed LLM turn or full gateway RPC end to end. The live terminal proof above drives the production session-manager resume/list boundary that the CLI/gateway resume-most-recent path depends on, with real on-disk JSONL session files and the colliding default session directory.

AI-assisted: yes. No agent transcript is included because explicit transcript approval was not requested during this fast contributor workflow.

target.title: fix(google): bound Gemini batch embedding file content reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces Gemini batch embedding output download parsing with streaming JSONL parsing instead of whole-file `res.text()` buffering.
- Allows valid batch output files larger than the generic 16 MiB text cap while bounding each individual JSONL record to 16 MiB.
- Intentionally out of scope: changing Gemini batch submission/polling behavior or adding live Gemini credentials.
- Review focus: the intentional per-record cap and the streamed output parsing strategy.

## Linked context

Closes #97687.

Related: provider response hardening series for successful provider/control-plane reads.

Maintainer request: not directly requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Gemini embedding batch output files were previously read with unbounded whole-body text parsing, which can OOM for large JSONL output files.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 using `runGeminiEmbeddingBatches` against a real `node:http` server bound to `127.0.0.1`; no fetch mock on the streaming proof cases.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/embedding-batch.test.ts -- --run -t "file-content streaming"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  2 passed | 4 skipped (6)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

- Observed result after fix: a valid local JSONL output file with 16,500 embedding records and total size above 16 MiB streams successfully; a single oversized JSONL record rejects with `gemini.batch-file-content: JSONL line exceeds 16777216 bytes`.
- What was not tested: a live Gemini batch run with real Google credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no live Gemini API key is available in this environment. The proof exercises the production batch runner and real HTTP transport locally, including upload/create/download routing.
- Before evidence: source on `main` downloaded the successful output file as one full text body before parsing JSONL.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/embedding-batch.test.ts -- --run -t "file-content streaming"`
- Existing bounded JSON response tests for upload/create/status paths remain in the same file.
- Regression coverage proves both desired outcomes: total output larger than 16 MiB succeeds, but one oversized record fails closed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. A single JSONL output record above 16 MiB now fails closed.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, provider response streaming/size enforcement changed.

What is the highest-risk area? A provider-valid single embedding record above 16 MiB would now fail.

How is that risk mitigated? Normal embedding records are far below 16 MiB, and large total batch output remains supported by streaming line-by-line instead of capping the whole file.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper/maintainer re-review after this template/proof refresh.

Waiting on: maintainer acceptance of the 16 MiB per-JSONL-record guard or request for a different record cap.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: the body now states that the cap is per line, not whole file, and documents the missing live Gemini proof as an environment limitation.

_AI-assisted; implementation and validation reviewed before pushing._

target.title: fix(ui): localize skill workshop switcher
target.body:
Splits out the i18n portion of #79745.

This PR localizes the Skill Workshop mode switcher (Board / Today) into all supported Control UI locales. It does not touch the qmd/mcporter memory path.

## Verification

Rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`ded3a93058`) and regenerated Control UI i18n output from `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts` with fallback-only sync. The generated non-English locale bundles now keep the five new `skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.*` keys as English fallbacks until translators run.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Skill Workshop Board/Today switcher in Control UI now renders through i18n instead of hard-coded English strings, matching the existing localized `skillWorkshop.header.*` pattern.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, Node 24.15.0, branch `fix/ui-skill-workshop-mode-switcher-i18n` at `dc147ebfb5`. Translation provider keys were unset for i18n generation/check, so new non-English keys intentionally fall back to English.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  env -u OPENAI_API_KEY -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts sync --write
  env -u OPENAI_API_KEY -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/i18n/test/skill-workshop-mode-switcher.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.skill-workshop.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
  node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/i18n/test/skill-workshop-mode-switcher.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.skill-workshop.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts
  node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` passed: `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 22 passed (22)`.
  - Rendered proof test emits the actual `.sw-mode-switch` from `renderApp` in `de` locale with `aria-label="Workshop view"`, `title="Board view"`, `title="Today view"`, visible `Board`, visible `Today`, and active Board `aria-selected="true"`.
  - `node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check` passed: all 18 non-English locales clean with `fallbacks=33`.
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...` passed: all matched files use the correct format.
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8` passed: core, extensions, and scripts lint shards finished without findings.
- **Observed result after fix:** `renderSkillWorkshopHeaderControls` resolves all five switcher strings through `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.*")`. The `en.ts` source defines those keys; generated non-English locale bundles include the same fallback values and metadata. The rendered Skill Workshop switcher still exposes the same tab roles, button titles, visible labels, and active-state ARIA attributes.
- **What was not tested:** Live browser screenshot in a non-English Control UI session; screen-reader announcement of the new `aria-label`; translator-supplied localized strings after a future translation pass.

## Related

- Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent #79745
- qmd-manager leaks XDG_CONFIG_HOME / XDG_CACHE_HOME to all child spawns, breaking mcporter ≥ 0.10 integration #79847

target.title: fix(zai): send current GLM thinking params
target.body:
Closes #97772

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users enable reasoning on Z.ai GLM models but OpenClaw sends the stale `enable_thinking` payload field, so current GLM reasoning mode is not activated for GLM-5.2 and no `reasoning_content` is returned.

## Why This Change Was Made

The OpenAI-compatible completions formatter now emits the current Z.ai thinking payload shape, `thinking: { type: "enabled" | "disabled" }`, instead of the legacy `enable_thinking` field. GLM-5.2 also receives the provider-supported `reasoning_effort` values (`high` or `max`), while binary Z.ai thinking models such as GLM-5.1 keep the on/off payload without an unsupported effort field.

## User Impact

Z.ai GLM-5.2 users can turn reasoning on and have OpenClaw send the request shape expected by the current Z.ai API. Existing binary thinking models keep their current behavior and do not receive extra unsupported `reasoning_effort` parameters.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: the Z.ai OpenAI-compatible request body uses `thinking.type`, never `enable_thinking`; GLM-5.2 maps enabled low-style levels to `reasoning_effort: "high"`, strongest OpenClaw effort to `"max"`, disables with `thinking: { type: "disabled" }`, and does not add `reasoning_effort` for binary GLM-5.1.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` -> 38 tests passed.
  - `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/types.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
  - `git diff --check` -> no whitespace errors.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Observed result: focused payload tests intercept the real `streamOpenAICompletions` request body before network and verify the current Z.ai thinking fields and effort gating.
- Not tested / proof gaps: no live Z.ai API call was run from this environment, so this PR does not include a real GLM-5.2 response containing `reasoning_content`.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: yes. Codex inspected the issue, checked duplicate PR coverage, implemented the focused fix, ran validation, and addressed autoreview findings before opening this PR.

target.title: fix(cron): warn when toolsAllow includes web_search but no search provider is enabled
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a cron preflight diagnostic when `toolsAllow` requests `web_search` but no web search provider plugin is enabled.
- Keeps execution behavior unchanged: the job still runs and delivery/status are not blocked.
- Makes the misconfiguration visible in diagnostics instead of leaving operators with a successful run that could not actually search.
- Review focus: diagnostic-only behavior and the plugin/provider availability check.

## Linked context

Closes #97654.

Related: cron isolated-agent diagnostics and tool availability handling.

Maintainer request: not directly requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: cron jobs could allow `web_search` while all search provider plugins were disabled, producing no operator-visible warning.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw cron isolated-agent test harness on Node 22 with web search providers mocked to an empty list.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts -- --run -t "warns when web_search is allowed but no provider is enabled"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  1 passed | 3 skipped (4)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

- Observed result after fix: cron diagnostics include a warn-severity message explaining that `web_search` was requested but no provider plugin is enabled, while the run status remains unchanged.
- What was not tested: a live scheduled cron run through a deployed gateway.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof uses the cron isolated-agent harness with runtime provider list controlled to reproduce the misconfiguration deterministically.
- Before evidence: the same configuration completed without a diagnostics warning.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts -- --run -t "warns when web_search is allowed but no provider is enabled"`
- `/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.test-harness.ts`
- Regression coverage verifies diagnostic text, severity, and that passthrough execution behavior remains intact.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Operators now see an additional warning diagnostic.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No execution behavior change; only preflight diagnostics change.

What is the highest-risk area? False-positive warning if provider availability is not loaded before the preflight.

How is that risk mitigated? The check runs after runtime plugins are loaded and uses the same provider listing path used by web search availability.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper/maintainer re-review after the previous infrastructure timeout.

Waiting on: ClawSweeper to complete a fresh review; the earlier review failed due infrastructure timeout, not patch quality.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: lint issue fixed with `Set.has`, focused regression coverage added, and PR body now follows the full template.

_AI-assisted; implementation and validation reviewed before pushing._

target.title: fix(matrix): bound raw transport response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

- Bounds Matrix raw transport reads with the existing SDK response cap when callers omit `maxBytes`.
- Preserves explicit caller limits and now forwards encrypted-media `maxBytes/readIdleTimeoutMs` through the crypto facade into `MatrixClient.downloadContent`.
- Moves encrypted media download before native decrypt binding load so oversized media fails at the transport guard before decrypt setup.
- Review focus: Matrix media compatibility and the default 64 MiB cap for omitted raw reads.

## Linked context

Closes #97644.

Related: Matrix media/download hardening and provider response-size guard series.

Maintainer request: not directly requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Matrix raw responses with omitted `maxBytes` could buffer unbounded data; encrypted media options were also dropped before transport.
- Real environment tested: local Matrix extension tests on Node 22 using real HTTP transport paths and a real `node:http` server for raw response cap proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.test.ts -- --run -t "preserves encrypted media download limits through the crypto facade"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  1 passed | 84 skipped (85)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

Earlier focused transport proof on this branch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts -- --run
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  16 passed (16)
```

- Observed result after fix: encrypted media download limits reach the transport layer and reject oversized media with `Matrix media exceeds configured size limit`; raw omitted-`maxBytes` reads use the SDK default cap instead of `response.arrayBuffer()`.
- What was not tested: a live Matrix homeserver media download with private room media.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof is local transport/runtime proof with deterministic HTTP servers and SDK tests, not a live Matrix server capture.
- Before evidence: the crypto facade called `downloadContent(mxcUrl)` without forwarding opts, and raw omitted-`maxBytes` transport reads were unbounded.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.test.ts -- --run -t "preserves encrypted media download limits through the crypto facade"`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts -- --run`
- `/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/crypto-facade.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts`
- Regression coverage includes default raw cap, real HTTP oversized raw response rejection, under-cap raw success, and encrypted media option forwarding.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Oversized omitted-limit raw Matrix responses now fail closed.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, Matrix response body handling is stricter.

What is the highest-risk area? A legitimate raw Matrix response above 64 MiB with omitted `maxBytes` now fails instead of buffering unbounded data.

How is that risk mitigated? Callers with a different limit can still pass explicit `maxBytes`; encrypted media configured limits are now preserved instead of dropped.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper/maintainer re-review after code follow-up and body proof refresh.

Waiting on: maintainer acceptance of the omitted-raw-read default cap and local proof in place of live Matrix media proof.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: forwarded encrypted-media `maxBytes/readIdleTimeoutMs` into `MatrixClient.downloadContent` and added focused regression coverage for that path.

_AI-assisted; implementation and validation reviewed before pushing._

target.title: fix(pdf): bound AI provider response reads in PDF text extraction
target.body:
## Summary

- Bounds native PDF provider success JSON reads for Anthropic and Gemini with the shared provider JSON response limit instead of bare `res.json()`.
- Prevents a misbehaving or proxying AI provider endpoint from making PDF extraction buffer an arbitrarily large success body before parsing.
- Intentionally out of scope: changing provider output semantics, adding live Anthropic/Gemini credentials, or redesigning large-PDF extraction streaming.
- Review focus: the 16 MiB fail-closed compatibility tradeoff for native PDF success responses.

## Linked context

Closes #97688.

Related: existing provider response hardening series that routes success/control-plane provider reads through bounded helpers.

Maintainer request: not directly requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: native PDF provider success responses were read with unbounded `res.json()` in `anthropicAnalyzePdf` and `geminiAnalyzePdf`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 using the actual exported native PDF provider functions and a real `node:http` server bound to `127.0.0.1`; no fetch mock on these proof cases.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts -- --run -t "response-size guard"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  4 passed | 15 skipped (19)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

- Observed result after fix: under-cap local Anthropic/Gemini JSON responses parse and return extracted text; oversized 20 MiB success responses reject with `JSON response exceeds` before the server finishes streaming all chunks.
- What was not tested: live Anthropic or live Gemini API calls with real provider credentials; no private provider token is available in this environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a real local HTTP transport proof through the production provider functions, not a live third-party API proof. Maintainers still need to accept the 16 MiB success-response cap or request a native-PDF-specific cap.
- Before evidence: source on `main` used bare `res.json()` for both native PDF success paths, which buffers the response body to completion before JSON parsing.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts -- --run -t "response-size guard"`
- Regression coverage added for Anthropic and Gemini under-cap success bodies plus oversized streaming success bodies from a real local HTTP server.
- Existing request-shape/error tests remain in `src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts`.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Success responses above the shared JSON cap now fail closed instead of buffering unbounded data.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, network response body handling is stricter for two AI provider calls.

What is the highest-risk area? Legitimate native PDF outputs above 16 MiB would now fail.

How is that risk mitigated? Native PDF extraction defaults to `max_tokens` around normal text output sizes, and the failure mode is explicit instead of process OOM. The PR calls out the cap for maintainer decision.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper/maintainer re-review after the PR body proof refresh.

Waiting on: maintainer acceptance of the 16 MiB native PDF success-response cap or a requested cap adjustment.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: the PR body now uses the full template and explicitly separates real local HTTP proof from the missing live provider proof.

_AI-assisted; implementation and validation reviewed before pushing._

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agent/provider replay could receive literal placeholders such as `(see attached image)` or empty-output fallbacks instead of normal text/JSON tool results when a tool result block was structured but not typed as `text`.

This replacement PR covers the provider conversion paths from #96895 and the active OpenAI Responses transport path noted in review.

Closes #96857
Related: #96895

## Why This Change Was Made

Several replay paths only collected tool-result blocks with `type: "text"`. Structured non-media blocks could therefore be treated as empty output and collapse to an image/no-output fallback. This PR routes affected replay paths through one shared structured text extractor so text-like structured tool output reaches provider/model context as readable text.

Covered replay paths:

- OpenAI Responses shared conversion
- OpenAI-compatible chat completions conversion
- OpenAI Responses managed transport replay
- Google/Gemini conversion
- Anthropic tool-result replay
- Mistral tool-result replay

Current fallback behavior is preserved:

- empty non-image output remains `(no output)` / provider-equivalent no-output text where that fallback already exists;
- image-only output remains an image placeholder or image input part according to the provider path;
- media-only `image`, `image_url`, `input_image`, and `audio` blocks are not serialized into provider-bound text;
- structured non-media output is serialized as JSON text instead of being dropped by text-only filtering.

## Review Updates In Current Head

Current head: `04b044826a206e957449307bc87744b191f86bf8`.

This update addresses the latest review findings by:

- rebasing the branch onto current `main` and preserving the empty non-image no-output fallback;
- wiring the structured extractor through Anthropic and Mistral replay instead of leaving them as text-only siblings;
- reusing OpenClaw's shared structured secret redaction contract via `redactSecrets()` before provider replay;
- skipping media-only block types: `image`, `image_url`, `input_image`, and `audio`;
- omitting MIME-tagged binary `data` before provider/model replay, including `mime_type`, `mimeType`, `media_type`, `mediaType`, `content_type`, and `contentType` aliases;
- redacting actual inline data URIs while preserving ordinary prose such as `metadata:ready` and `data: is ordinary prose`;
- omitting opaque/binary fields such as `encrypted_content`, `encrypted_stdout`, `blob`, `buffer`, and `bytes`;
- adding focused helper/provider regression coverage.

## User Impact

Agents can inspect structured text-like tool output in model context instead of losing it to an attachment placeholder. Existing text output and media handling remain intact, and sensitive/binary/opaque fields are redacted or omitted before external provider replay.

## Evidence

- Live issue evidence: #96857 includes current redacted observations where ordinary text/status tool outputs reached agent-visible context as literal `(see attached image)` placeholders instead of text.
- Code-path evidence: current `main` filtered tool-result content down to `type: "text"` in the affected provider/transport paths; this patch routes those paths through a shared structured-text extractor.
- Provider scope evidence in this branch:
  - `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` covers structured tool results and current empty/image fallback behavior.
  - `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` covers structured Responses replay plus no-output/image fallback behavior.
  - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` covers managed OpenAI Responses transport replay for structured tool output.
  - `src/llm/providers/google-shared.convert.test.ts` covers Google/Gemini function-response structured replay.
  - `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts` covers Anthropic structured non-image replay and structured text alongside images.
  - `src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts` covers Mistral structured non-image replay and structured-only fallback behavior.
  - `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts` covers shared redaction, media-only skip, MIME-tagged binary omission, inline data URI redaction, opaque/binary field omission, and untruncated structured payloads.

Redacted provider-replay proof summary:

```text
OpenAI Responses: structured `json` tool result becomes function_call_output text containing `price`/`42`, not `(see attached image)`.
OpenAI completions: structured tool result becomes tool message text, while empty non-image output remains `(no output)` and image-bearing output remains image-specific handling.
Google/Gemini: structured tool result is serialized into function response text instead of being dropped by text-only filtering.
Anthropic: structured non-image tool_result content is serialized as JSON text; structured text can accompany image tool results without serializing media-only blocks as JSON text.
Mistral: structured non-image and structured-only tool results replay as text blocks instead of `(no tool output)`.
Shared extractor: secret-like structured fields are redacted via `redactSecrets()`, media-only blocks are skipped, binary/opaque fields are omitted, and inline data URI payloads are replaced with length-only placeholders.
```

Validation performed on current head:

```text
node --check src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.ts
node --check src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.ts src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot
```

Result:

```text
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 6 passed (6)
```

GitHub Actions evidence for current head:

- `Real behavior proof`: passed — https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28375724358/job/84064711765
- Full PR CI matrix for `04b044826a206e957449307bc87744b191f86bf8` is running/settling; at the time of this update there were no failed checks reported for the current head.

## Risk

- Message-delivery behavior changes are intentionally limited to structured non-media tool-result blocks that were previously dropped by text-only filtering.
- Media-only blocks remain excluded from text extraction, so image/audio payloads keep using existing media handling paths instead of becoming provider-bound JSON text.
- Shared structured redaction runs before provider replay, and binary/opaque fields are omitted before serialization.
- The helper remains untruncated at the provider conversion boundary; existing context/tool-result budgeting and truncation guards remain responsible for payload sizing.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous per-provider text-only extraction behavior. No migration, dependency, workflow, or persistent data changes are included.

## Not Tested / Limitations

- No live external provider API request was made by this update.
- Runtime proof is based on redacted provider-payload replay/converter paths and CI-visible checks, not on private credentials or non-public endpoints.

target.title: fix: report pending memory close errors
target.body:
Closes #96766

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where memory close could silently swallow failures from pending sync or provider initialization work, leaving shutdown-time memory/index problems invisible.

## Why This Change Was Made

`awaitPendingManagerWork` now accepts an error-reporting callback while preserving the existing close behavior of waiting without throwing those pending-work failures. `MemoryIndexManager.close()` wires that callback into the existing memory logger with close-specific messages for pending sync and provider init failures.

## User Impact

Operators now get diagnostics when close-time memory sync or provider initialization work fails. Shutdown behavior remains compatible: close still drains pending work and continues provider/database teardown instead of turning these background failures into new thrown close errors.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.async-search.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` (5 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` (54 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-async-state.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.async-search.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --codex-bin .artifacts/codex-flex` (clean, confidence 0.86)

Known proof gap: I did not run a live shutdown scenario forcing a real SQLite lock, disk-full, or provider-init failure; the defect and fix are source-level and regression-test covered.

target.title: Add boundary safety guard for response reads and truncation
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a boundary safety guard that inventories existing raw UTF-16 text truncation and unbounded response-body reads, then blocks newly introduced production-file violations in changed-file checks.
- Wire the guard into `pnpm check`, `pnpm check:changed`, and package scripts with a baseline so existing debt stays visible without blocking unrelated PRs.
- Fix the shared response snippet helper and representative ACP/MiniMax/provider call sites to use bounded response reads and UTF-16-safe truncation.

## Origin / follow-up

Follow-up to #97620. That PR fixed one unbounded provider response-read path. This follow-up addresses the recurring root pattern behind the recent boundary-fix cluster by adding a CI guard for new high-confidence response-body and UTF-16 text-truncation bypasses instead of continuing to rely only on one-off call-site fixes.

## Maintainer-ready notes

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Recent boundary fixes have repeatedly patched individual channel/provider call sites for the same two failure classes: user-visible text truncated through raw UTF-16 code units, and external HTTP response bodies read without a byte cap. Without an automated guard, new provider/channel/plugin code can reintroduce the same failure class after the canonical helpers already exist.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause CI/tooling guard for boundary safety regressions, plus small canonical-helper hardening.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the local CI/tooling enforcement path and touched helper/call-site regressions. The proof is intentionally scoped to repository guard behavior and local `Response` stream boundaries, not remote service availability.
- **Root cause:** The source invariant was missing from CI: new production code could still bypass OpenClaw's canonical UTF-16 and bounded-response helpers even though those helpers existed. A few shared helpers and representative call sites also still contained raw response reads or raw head truncation, so the repository had no enforced contract that new boundary code must use the canonical owners.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This fixes the root/source invariant instead of another sibling symptom because `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs` scans production TypeScript with the TypeScript AST, compares findings to a checked-in baseline, and fails only on new changed-file violations. It catches high-confidence raw string head truncation and awaited external `Response` body reads while avoiding tests, fixtures, generated/vendor code, Express response writers, hash/protocol slices, and collection slicing. Baseline identity is stable across line-only movement by comparing file/rule/match counts instead of treating line numbers as part of the violation identity. `scripts/check-changed.mjs` runs the guard for core/extension changes, while `scripts/check.mjs` checks full baseline drift.
- **What did NOT change:** This does not change broad runtime behavior, rewrite all legacy baseline entries, add new public APIs, change response-size defaults outside the touched MiniMax success path, or modify credentials/configuration. Full legacy remediation and exhaustive taint analysis are out of scope.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs` is the source-of-truth validator for new boundary-safety violations; `test/fixtures/boundary-safety-inventory.json` is the baseline contract for current legacy inventory; `@openclaw/media-core/read-response-with-limit` remains the canonical bounded response helper; `src/shared/utf16-slice.ts` remains the canonical UTF-16-safe truncation owner for runtime code.
- **Patch quality notes:** The guard is intentionally high-confidence rather than exhaustive: it prevents new clear bypasses without forcing noisy legacy cleanup. The baseline currently contains 273 known entries on this branch and can be updated only through the explicit `--all --update-baseline` command after intentional migrations. Round 2 addresses ClawSweeper's baseline-identity finding by keeping known findings stable when only their line number changes. Related open PR scan: exact searches for `boundary/response-body-limit`, `boundary/text-utf16-truncation`, and `check-boundary-safety` found no open PR already adding these rule names.
- **Target test file:** `test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts`, `packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts`, `src/agents/minimax-vlm.boundary.test.ts`, and `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** New raw `messageText.slice(0, maxChars - 1)` text truncation is flagged; `truncateUtf16Safe(...)` is accepted; collection/hash/protocol slices are ignored; awaited `response.json()` / `res.text().catch(...)` reads are flagged; Express `res.json({...})` writers are ignored; a baseline-known finding remains accepted when only its line number moves; the changed-file plan includes `pnpm check:boundary-safety` for core/extension changes.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression guard targets only high-confidence production-file sinks and leaves existing findings in a baseline, so it protects the source invariant for new code without requiring an unrelated legacy cleanup PR or noisy whole-repo rewrite.

## Root Cause

The root cause is a missing repository-level source invariant for boundary code. User-visible text can still be shortened with raw UTF-16 code-unit slicing, which can leave dangling surrogate halves when the cut lands inside an emoji or other surrogate-pair character. Remote HTTP responses can still be read with `response.json()`, `response.text()`, or `response.arrayBuffer()` directly, which buffers the entire body before OpenClaw can enforce the intended byte budget.

The canonical helpers existed, but the repository did not have a changed-file guard that forced new production code toward those helpers. As a result, each fix landed as a local call-site patch, while the next integration change could reintroduce the same boundary bypass in a sibling area. The shared response snippet path also still carried a raw no-stream fallback and raw character slicing, so even helper consumers were not fully protected.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** New production code that bypasses UTF-16-safe text truncation or bounded response-body helpers is now caught by the changed-file guard, while existing legacy findings remain tracked in a full-repo baseline.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw repair worktree at `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774`, branch `repair/pr-97774`, current PR #97774 head. The exercised behavior is repository CI/tooling enforcement plus local `Response` stream boundary handling.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm check:boundary-safety:all
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm check:boundary-safety
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm check:changed --dry-run
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts src/agents/minimax-vlm.boundary.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts -t "truncates parented task progress"
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm tsgo:core
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm tsgo:core:test
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm lint:scripts
```

```bash
git -C /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
$ node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --all
Boundary safety baseline matches (273 entries).
```

```text
$ node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs
Boundary safety changed-file check passed (0 changed-file entries, all baseline-known).
```

```text
$ node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json
[]
Boundary safety changed-file check passed (0 changed-file entries, all baseline-known).
```

```text
[check:changed:dry-run] lanes=core, coreTests, tooling
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:database-first-legacy-stores
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:media-download-helpers
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:runtime-sidecar-loaders
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:boundary-safety
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:import-cycles
```

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)
```

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards.
packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts: 12 tests passed.
src/agents/minimax-vlm.boundary.test.ts: 1 test passed.
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts -t "truncates parented task progress" exited 0.
```

```text
pnpm tsgo:core exited 0.
pnpm tsgo:core:test exited 0.
pnpm lint:scripts exited 0.
git diff --check exited 0 with no whitespace errors.
```

```text
check-upstream-fixed: CONTINUE_ALLOWED; upstream_fixed=false; stop_required=false; sync_before_fix_required=false.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The full guard baseline matches 273 known legacy entries on the current PR repair worktree, while the changed-file guard passes because the patch itself introduces no new unbaseline-known violations. `node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json` returns an empty changed-file inventory, and the focused tooling test now includes the line-shift baseline identity regression. `pnpm check:changed --dry-run` includes `pnpm check:boundary-safety` for this core/tooling change, proving the guard is wired into the changed-file path. The touched helper/call-site regressions verify UTF-16-safe snippet/progress truncation and bounded MiniMax success response reads.
- **What was not tested:** No browser UI or hosted CI run was exercised locally. Remote MiniMax availability is not claimed here; the MiniMax change is covered at the local fetch/`Response` boundary, which is the behavior changed by replacing raw `res.json()` with the bounded JSON reader.

## Review findings addressed

- **Proof request / real behavior evidence:** The reviewer-facing proof is intentionally mapped to the actual behavior changed here: repository guard execution, baseline diffing, and local `Response` stream boundary handling. No browser UI, hosted CI rerun, or remote MiniMax availability is claimed; local chat/RPC/Control UI proof would not exercise the changed CI/tooling boundary more directly than the recorded script runs and local `Response` stream tests.
- **RF-001 — status label:** Expected to clear after this round 2 update is pushed and ClawSweeper re-evaluates the PR; no code change is tied to the label itself.
- **RF-002 — baseline line-shift defect:** Fixed in `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs` by comparing baseline entries with stable file/rule/match identity counts instead of line-number-inclusive keys.
- **RF-003 — MiniMax 4 MiB cap policy:** Maintainer decision. The PR body now explicitly calls out that MiniMax VLM successful responses over 4 MiB fail closed via the bounded JSON reader and that this provider-compatibility tradeoff should be maintainer-accepted before merge.
- **RF-004 — unrelated PR block from line shifts:** Fixed by the same stable baseline identity change; a touched file with a baseline-known violation that only moved line numbers no longer appears as a new violation.
- **RF-005 — MiniMax success-response compatibility:** Maintainer decision. This remains intentional fail-closed behavior for unbounded provider success bodies, with the compatibility risk disclosed in Merge risk.
- **RF-006 — repair split:** Fixed the focused script/test baseline-identity issue; the MiniMax cap remains explicitly disclosed as maintainer compatibility policy.
- **RF-007 — stable baseline findings:** Fixed in `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs`; line numbers remain in output/baseline for diagnostics but no longer define violation identity.
- **RF-008 — requested tooling test:** Passed: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts` with 10 tests.
- **RF-009 — requested JSON check:** Passed: `node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json` returned `[]` and exited successfully.
- **RF-010 — requested full baseline check:** Passed: `pnpm check:boundary-safety:all` / `node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --all` reported `Boundary safety baseline matches (273 entries).`
- **RF-011 — requested changed dry-run:** Passed: `pnpm check:changed --dry-run` includes `pnpm check:boundary-safety`.

## Regression Test Plan

- `test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts` locks guard detection and false-positive boundaries for string truncation, response-body reads, Express writers, collection slices, baseline diffing, and line-shift-stable baseline identity.
- `packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts` locks bounded response snippet behavior and UTF-16-safe snippet truncation.
- `src/agents/minimax-vlm.boundary.test.ts` locks success-response overflow cancellation.
- `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts` locks parented ACP task progress truncation without dangling surrogate halves.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** CI/tooling, boundary safety, provider response reads, UTF-16 text truncation.
- **Risk explanation:** The guard can block new changed-file code that uses raw response reads or raw string truncation in production files. It should not block unrelated legacy debt because existing entries are baseline-known by stable file/rule/match identity, not by fragile line numbers, and it intentionally excludes tests/fixtures/generated/vendor files plus common false positives. MiniMax VLM success responses over 4 MiB now fail closed through the bounded provider JSON reader instead of fully buffering the body; that provider-compatibility tradeoff is intentional and should be maintainer-accepted before merge.
- **Why acceptable:** The failure mode is actionable: use `truncateUtf16Safe`, `sliceUtf16Safe`, `readResponseWithLimit`, `readResponseTextSnippet`, `readProviderJsonResponse`, or add a narrow `boundary-safety-ignore <rule-id>: <reason>` for a proven false positive. This turns a repeated review/bug pattern into a local CI failure before PR submission.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This PR does not attempt full legacy remediation, exhaustive taint analysis, or live third-party provider/channel validation.

target.title: fix: preserve Feishu post line breaks
target.body:
Closes #97074

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users sending Feishu post/markdown messages with ordinary single newlines would see those lines collapsed together as cramped text instead of readable separated lines.

## Why This Change Was Made

Feishu post `md` elements need single prose line breaks materialized before payload creation, while fenced code blocks need their original single newlines preserved. The send and edit paths also resolve the Feishu text limit and skip materialization when the rendered post would exceed that limit, so existing chunked callers do not start sending oversized messages.

## User Impact

Feishu post replies with normal multiline prose render with the intended spacing. Code blocks remain compact, and near-limit messages keep the existing bounded send behavior instead of being expanded past the configured Feishu limit.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-target.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` (86 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/send-target.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-result.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.ts extensions/feishu/src/post-markdown.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --codex-bin .artifacts/codex-flex` (clean, confidence 0.84)

Known proof gap: I did not run a live Feishu API send because this workspace does not have approved live Feishu credentials for this issue sweep.

target.title: fix: make tool loop circuit breaker session-global
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the global tool loop circuit breaker so it tracks a session-wide consecutive no-progress streak instead of only repeated calls to the same tool+args pair.

Fixes #79252

## What Problem This Solves

`globalCircuitBreakerThreshold` is documented as the final global no-progress breaker, but current main derives it from `getNoProgressStreak(history, toolName, currentHash)`. That means a runaway session can avoid the global breaker by alternating tools or arguments while receiving the same no-progress outcome each time. Each per-tool/per-args streak stays below the threshold even though the session as a whole is stuck.

## Issue / Motivation

Issue #79252 reports a real incident where a session cycled through multiple tools after each individual tool was blocked, consuming many model calls and tokens before manual intervention. The intended behavior is for the global breaker to count consecutive no-progress outcomes across the scoped session, independent of the specific tool signature.

## Changes

- Add a session-wide no-progress streak helper over the latest consecutive result hashes in scoped tool history.
- Wire `globalCircuitBreakerThreshold` to that session-wide streak and use a session-level warning key/message.
- Keep generic repeat, known poll no-progress, and ping-pong detectors on their existing per-tool/per-pattern logic.
- Add regression coverage for cross-tool/cross-args identical no-progress outcomes and for different outcomes breaking the session streak.

## Evidence

The regression test now records four consecutive successful calls using different tools and different args (`read`, `list`, `search`, `inspect`) with the same no-progress result, then verifies that the next tool call is blocked by `global_circuit_breaker` with count `4`. A paired negative test records a different result in the middle and verifies the session-global breaker does not fire.

Local validation completed on this branch:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
✓ agents  src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts (58 tests) 146ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  58 passed (58)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.99s

$ pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 20ms on 2 files using 1 threads.

$ git diff --check
(no output)

$ git diff origin/main...HEAD | added-line secret scan
secret_scan_ok
```

The previous Real behavior proof failures were PR-body metadata failures only: the workflow required authored `What Problem This Solves` and `Evidence` sections. This update adds those sections without changing code.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts`
- `pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Added-line secret scan over `git diff origin/main...HEAD`



## Follow-up validation (2026-06-29)

Addressed ClawSweeper feedback by aggregating stable no-progress outcomes per tool/args pattern across the session tail instead of requiring every adjacent call to share one result hash. Added coverage for stable read/list tails, unique progress-only tails, changed repeated-pattern output, progress after a stable tail, and earlier unique progress before a stable tail.

Commands run on commit `74ebbadb507a6ff93a69ec4b0bd99a33c88012e1`:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts -- --run` — 1 shard passed; 61 tests passed
- `pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check` and `git diff --cached --check` — passed
- added-line secret scan over `git diff origin/main...HEAD` — clean

target.title: fix(google): bound Gemini batch embedding file content reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces Gemini batch embedding output download parsing with streaming JSONL parsing instead of whole-file `res.text()` buffering.
- Allows valid batch output files larger than the generic 16 MiB text cap while bounding each individual JSONL record to 16 MiB.
- Intentionally out of scope: changing Gemini batch submission/polling behavior or adding live Gemini credentials.
- Review focus: the intentional per-record cap and the streamed output parsing strategy.

## Linked context

Closes #97687.

Related: provider response hardening series for successful provider/control-plane reads.

Maintainer request: not directly requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Gemini embedding batch output files were previously read with unbounded whole-body text parsing, which can OOM for large JSONL output files.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 using `runGeminiEmbeddingBatches` against a real `node:http` server bound to `127.0.0.1`; no fetch mock on the streaming proof cases.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/embedding-batch.test.ts -- --run -t "file-content streaming"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  2 passed | 4 skipped (6)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

- Observed result after fix: a valid local JSONL output file with 16,500 embedding records and total size above 16 MiB streams successfully; a single oversized JSONL record rejects with `gemini.batch-file-content: JSONL line exceeds 16777216 bytes`.
- What was not tested: a live Gemini batch run with real Google credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no live Gemini API key is available in this environment. The proof exercises the production batch runner and real HTTP transport locally, including upload/create/download routing.
- Before evidence: source on `main` downloaded the successful output file as one full text body before parsing JSONL.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/embedding-batch.test.ts -- --run -t "file-content streaming"`
- Existing bounded JSON response tests for upload/create/status paths remain in the same file.
- Regression coverage proves both desired outcomes: total output larger than 16 MiB succeeds, but one oversized record fails closed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. A single JSONL output record above 16 MiB now fails closed.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, provider response streaming/size enforcement changed.

What is the highest-risk area? A provider-valid single embedding record above 16 MiB would now fail.

How is that risk mitigated? Normal embedding records are far below 16 MiB, and large total batch output remains supported by streaming line-by-line instead of capping the whole file.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper/maintainer re-review after this template/proof refresh.

Waiting on: maintainer acceptance of the 16 MiB per-JSONL-record guard or request for a different record cap.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: the body now states that the cap is per line, not whole file, and documents the missing live Gemini proof as an environment limitation.

_AI-assisted; implementation and validation reviewed before pushing._

target.title: fix(claude-cli): surface re-auth hint when subprocess OAuth token expires
target.body:
## Summary

- Surfaces a targeted Claude CLI re-auth hint when the subprocess OAuth token expires and returns a 401 authentication failure.
- Handles both message-based failures and structured `FailoverError` metadata where `provider: "claude-cli"` is not embedded in the display message.
- Emits the correct recovery command: `openclaw models auth login --provider anthropic --method cli`.
- Review focus: the narrowness of the Claude CLI classification and precedence over generic provider-auth copy.

## Linked context

Fixes #97553.

Related: #97561 overlaps the same root cause; this branch now covers the structured `FailoverError` path called out by ClawSweeper.

Maintainer request: not directly requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: expired Claude CLI OAuth failures could surface generic provider-auth guidance instead of the actionable Claude CLI login command.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 using the agent-runner failure path with typed `FailoverError` inputs that match the subprocess 401 envelope.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -- --run -t "claude-cli"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests covered in this focused run:
- classifies claude-cli subprocess 401 OAuth expiry as a provider refresh failure
- classifies structured claude-cli 401 failures even when the display message omits the provider
- surfaces claude-cli re-auth hint over generic provider auth copy for 401 OAuth expiry
- surfaces claude-cli re-auth hint from structured provider metadata when the message omits claude-cli
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards
```

- Observed result after fix: the full `runAgentTurnWithFallback` path returns `⚠️ Model login expired on the gateway for claude-cli. Re-auth with \`openclaw models auth login --provider anthropic --method cli\`, then try again.`
- What was not tested: a live expired Claude CLI OAuth session, because this environment does not intentionally hold an expired Claude CLI credential.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof uses the real OpenClaw failure mapping path with typed error envelopes, not a live Telegram/Mantis transcript.
- Before evidence: typed `reason:"auth"` + `status:401` could be caught by generic provider-auth classification before the targeted OAuth-refresh hint.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -- --run -t "claude-cli"`
- `/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- New regression coverage includes the structured provider metadata path requested by ClawSweeper.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. A specific Claude CLI 401 now gets a targeted re-auth hint.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, auth failure classification and recovery guidance changed.

What is the highest-risk area? Misclassifying unrelated provider 401 failures as Claude CLI OAuth expiry.

How is that risk mitigated? Structured classification requires `FailoverError`, `provider: "claude-cli"`, `reason: "auth"`, `status: 401`, plus the Claude authentication failure wording in the message or raw error.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper/maintainer re-review after the structured metadata fix.

Waiting on: maintainer decision on overlap with #97561; this branch now addresses the gap noted by ClawSweeper.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: handled the raw structured `claude-cli` `FailoverError` path where provider is not embedded in message text.

_AI-assisted; implementation and validation reviewed before pushing._

target.title: fix(control-ui): WebSocket connects to wrong gateway when two share an origin
target.body:
## Summary

- Keeps Control UI gateway settings isolated when two gateways share the same origin but use different base paths.
- Stops adopting same-origin sibling gateway settings from the legacy unscoped localStorage key.
- Preserves upgrade behavior for a legacy custom remote gateway URL on first load.
- Review focus: localStorage compatibility and the same-origin/different-basePath isolation rule.

## Linked context

Closes #97636.

Related: #97643 targets the same root cause but removed too much legacy migration behavior; this branch preserves remote custom gateway migration.

Maintainer request: not directly requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Gateway B could connect its WebSocket to Gateway A when both were served from the same origin under different base paths and B had no scoped settings yet.
- Real environment tested: local Control UI storage runtime test on Node 22 with browser-like `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, and `location` globals. The tests simulate `https://multi.example:8443/gateway-a/chat` and `https://multi.example:8443/gateway-b/chat` with separate saved settings.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts -- --run -t "gateway"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  25 passed (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

- Observed result after fix: Gateway B loads `wss://multi.example:8443/gateway-b`, its own UI preferences/session, and does not adopt Gateway A state; a legacy custom remote URL such as `wss://remote-gateway.example.com` is still migrated.
- What was not tested: a real browser screenshot/network panel capture against two deployed gateways.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a deterministic storage-layer proof, not a browser E2E screenshot. It directly covers the WebSocket target input used by the UI connection path.
- Before evidence: the legacy unscoped key could contain Gateway A's `gatewayUrl` and be accepted by Gateway B when B lacked a scoped key.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts -- --run -t "gateway"`
- `/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint ui/src/ui/storage.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
- Regression coverage includes sibling gateway isolation, no legacy unscoped writes, custom remote gateway reload persistence, and legacy custom remote migration.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Same-origin sibling gateway settings are no longer adopted from the legacy key.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Control UI localStorage fallback behavior changed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, WebSocket target selection is affected.

What is the highest-risk area? Existing users with legacy same-origin custom path settings could lose fallback behavior if it looked like sibling-gateway pollution.

How is that risk mitigated? Different-origin custom remote gateway URLs remain accepted and migrated; same-origin different-basePath legacy records are the reported broken state and are intentionally rejected.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper/maintainer re-review after body proof refresh.

Waiting on: maintainer acceptance that deterministic storage proof is sufficient, or request for browser/network screenshot proof.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: preserved and tested legacy custom remote gateway migration while blocking same-origin sibling basePath pollution.

_AI-assisted; implementation and validation reviewed before pushing._

target.title: fix(cron): warn when toolsAllow includes web_search but no search provider is enabled
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a cron preflight diagnostic when `toolsAllow` requests `web_search` but no web search provider plugin is enabled.
- Keeps execution behavior unchanged: the job still runs and delivery/status are not blocked.
- Makes the misconfiguration visible in diagnostics instead of leaving operators with a successful run that could not actually search.
- Review focus: diagnostic-only behavior and the plugin/provider availability check.

## Linked context

Closes #97654.

Related: cron isolated-agent diagnostics and tool availability handling.

Maintainer request: not directly requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: cron jobs could allow `web_search` while all search provider plugins were disabled, producing no operator-visible warning.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw cron isolated-agent test harness on Node 22 with web search providers mocked to an empty list.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts -- --run -t "warns when web_search is allowed but no provider is enabled"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  1 passed | 3 skipped (4)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

- Observed result after fix: cron diagnostics include a warn-severity message explaining that `web_search` was requested but no provider plugin is enabled, while the run status remains unchanged.
- What was not tested: a live scheduled cron run through a deployed gateway.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof uses the cron isolated-agent harness with runtime provider list controlled to reproduce the misconfiguration deterministically.
- Before evidence: the same configuration completed without a diagnostics warning.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts -- --run -t "warns when web_search is allowed but no provider is enabled"`
- `/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.test-harness.ts`
- Regression coverage verifies diagnostic text, severity, and that passthrough execution behavior remains intact.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Operators now see an additional warning diagnostic.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No execution behavior change; only preflight diagnostics change.

What is the highest-risk area? False-positive warning if provider availability is not loaded before the preflight.

How is that risk mitigated? The check runs after runtime plugins are loaded and uses the same provider listing path used by web search availability.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper/maintainer re-review after the previous infrastructure timeout.

Waiting on: ClawSweeper to complete a fresh review; the earlier review failed due infrastructure timeout, not patch quality.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: lint issue fixed with `Set.has`, focused regression coverage added, and PR body now follows the full template.

target.title: test(config): add unit tests for sensitive config path classification
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `sensitive-paths.ts` module classifies config paths whose values should be redacted from UI/API output. It uses pattern matching against common secret patterns (`token`, `password`, `secret`, `api.*key`, etc.), a whitelist for known non-sensitive suffixes that happen to match (`maxTokens`, `tokenLimit`, `passwordFile`), and a local service env override that is always treated as sensitive. Despite being a core path for config redaction, this function had no direct unit tests.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add focused unit tests for `isSensitiveConfigPath` to verify:
- All 7 sensitive regex patterns match correctly (case-insensitive, fully qualified paths)
- Whitelist exemptions apply correctly for all 10 suffixes (case-insensitive, no suffix leak)
- Local service env paths are always sensitive and take priority over whitelist
- Non-sensitive paths (model, temperature, etc.) are correctly rejected
- Composite/nested config paths are correctly classified

This improves test coverage and prevents regressions when patterns or whitelist are modified.

## User Impact

Config path sensitivity classification now has 33 dedicated unit tests covering the main branching paths, reducing the risk of regressions.

## Evidence

Redacted real config path proof via production `redactConfigObject` pipeline:

```
$ node --import tsx -e "... redactConfigObject() ..."

=== Input config (with secrets) ===
{
  "providers": {
    "openai": { "apiKey": "sk-abc123", "model": "gpt-4" },
    "anthropic": { "apiKey": "sk-ant-xyz789" }
  },
  "plugins": {
    "myapp": {
      "clientSecret": "s3cr3t!",
      "passwordFile": "/etc/passwd",
      "maxTokens": 8192,
      "enabled": true
    }
  },
  "channels": {
    "discord": { "token": "discord-token-xxx", "guildId": "12345" }
  }
}

=== After redactConfigObject() ===
{
  "providers": {
    "openai": { "apiKey": "__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__", "model": "gpt-4" },
    "anthropic": { "apiKey": "__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__" }
  },
  "plugins": {
    "myapp": {
      "clientSecret": "__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__",
      "passwordFile": "/etc/passwd",
      "maxTokens": 8192,
      "enabled": true
    }
  },
  "channels": {
    "discord": { "token": "__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__", "guildId": "12345" }
  }
}

=== Redaction summary ===
🔴 REDACTED   providers.openai.apiKey
🟢 KEPT       providers.openai.model                             "gpt-4"
🔴 REDACTED   providers.anthropic.apiKey
🔴 REDACTED   plugins.myapp.clientSecret
🟢 KEPT       plugins.myapp.passwordFile                         "/etc/passwd"
🟢 KEPT       plugins.myapp.maxTokens                            8192
🟢 KEPT       plugins.myapp.enabled                              true
🔴 REDACTED   channels.discord.token
🟢 KEPT       channels.discord.guildId                           "12345"
```

- `apiKey` → redacted, `model` → preserved (no pattern match)
- `clientSecret` → redacted, `passwordFile` → preserved (whitelisted suffix)
- `maxTokens` → preserved (whitelisted suffix), `token` → redacted
- `guildId` → preserved (no pattern match)

Direct helper probe:

```
$ node --import tsx -e "import('./src/config/sensitive-paths.js').then(({ isSensitiveConfigPath }) => console.log(JSON.stringify([...], null, 2)))"
[
  { "desc": "apiKey → sensitive", "result": true },
  { "desc": "botToken → sensitive", "result": true },
  { "desc": "password → sensitive", "result": true },
  { "desc": "maxTokens → not sensitive", "result": false },
  { "desc": "passwordFile → not sensitive", "result": false },
  { "desc": "localservice.env.X → sensitive", "result": true },
  { "desc": "model → not sensitive", "result": false }
]
```

Targeted test:

```
$ pnpm test src/config/sensitive-paths.test.ts

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 12.75s
```
